scope_boundary: synthetic security-kernel authority-use receipt discipline and finite Lean security predicates only; no kernel security, sandbox isolation, side-channel safety, prompt-injection containment, secret-handle safety, least-privilege context behavior, runtime policy behavior, source interpretation, or source-derived support-state promotion
transition_reason: The repository now has an authority-use receipt schema, a protocol fixture, finite SecurityKernel Lean predicates, proof adequacy review text that keeps the target narrow, exact source mappings for the Security Kernel chapter, and a synthetic security-kernel harness with valid and expected-invalid fixtures. Those artifacts make handle mediation, approval artifacts, bounded action scope, SCIF lifecycle completeness, sanitization, residual leak-risk notes, revocation paths, and prompt-injection non-disclosure boundaries executable at the record level. They still do not establish kernel security, sandbox isolation, side-channel safety, prompt-injection containment, secret-handle safety, least-privilege context behavior, runtime policy behavior, source interpretation adequacy, or deployed security.
required_artifacts: deployed security-kernel implementation with handle mediation over real privileged actions
sandbox or compartment isolation review with positive and negative controls
prompt-injection containment test over real adversarial prompts and secret handles
least-privilege context admission trace over real sensitive context
runtime revocation and lease-expiry test over actual authority handles
independent security, runtime, formal-methods, or source-interpretation review
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
verification_command: python3 scripts/validate_security_kernel.py && python3 scripts/validate_evidence_transitions.py && python3 scripts/validate_source_evidence_audit.py && python3 scripts/validate_v1_status_snapshot.py
negative_results: Security Kernel and Digital SCIFs remains at argument
schema validation checks authority-use receipt shape rather than security enforcement
finite Lean predicates remain useful but too narrow for kernel-security, sandbox-isolation, side-channel, or prompt-injection containment claims
secret-handle safety, least-privilege context behavior, runtime revocation, source interpretation review, and deployed security are not implemented
downgrade_triggers: chapter text interpreted as kernel-security or sandbox-isolation evidence
synthetic sanitized-output fixture interpreted as leak-prevention or side-channel safety evidence
prompt-injection fixture interpreted as runtime prompt-injection containment
finite Lean security predicates treated as deployed security-kernel behavior
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the synthetic receipt discipline that passed, or after deployed handle mediation, sandbox or compartment isolation review, side-channel and logging threat-model review, prompt-injection containment tests over real adversarial prompts, least-privilege context traces, runtime revocation tests, and independent security, runtime, formal-methods, or source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not prove that real secrets stay outside model context
schema validation does not prove sandbox isolation, SCIF isolation, or side-channel safety
finite Lean predicates do not implement a security kernel
sanitized-output strings do not prove leak prevention
prompt-injection fixture strings do not prove runtime prompt-injection containment
no independent security, runtime, formal-methods, or source-interpretation review has been recorded
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent security, runtime-systems, adversarial-prompting, formal-methods, side-channel, reviewer-quality, or source-interpretation audit
acceptance_blockers: no sandbox or compartment isolation review
no side-channel or logging threat-model review
no prompt-injection containment test over real adversarial prompts
no least-privilege context trace over real sensitive context
no runtime revocation or lease-expiry test over actual authority handles
no independent security, runtime, formal-methods, or source-interpretation review
non_claims: does not promote Security Kernel and Digital SCIFs above argument
does not prove sandbox isolation or side-channel safety
does not prove prompt-injection containment or secret-handle safety
does not prove least-privilege context behavior or runtime policy behavior
